content: read reviews/84.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/84.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/84.md.

    28→

    30→- [x] `cycleSetCount` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- The number of distinct cycle spectra ... -/`).

    33→- [x] Functions returning types use `lowerCamelCase` (`cycleSpectrum`, `cycleSetCount`).

    37→
    38→## 6. Theorem Statements
    39→
    40→- [x] Both statements use the `theorem` keyword.
    41→- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_84` (snake_case matching namespace number).
    42→- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**.
    43→- [x] Main theorem and variant both have docstrings.
    44→- [x] Docstrings reference source papers: main cites [Er94b][Er95][Er96][Er97d]; variant cites [Ve04].
    45→
    46→### `answer()` usage
    47→
    48→The problem asserts two conjectures as direct facts ("Erdős and Faudree conjectured that f(n) = o(2^n) and f(n)/2^{n/2} → ∞"). This is **Case 3** — direct assertions. No `answer()` wrapper is needed.
    49→
    50→- [x] Main theorem (`erdos_84`) states `f(n)/2^{n/2} → ∞` directly without `answer()`. Correct for Case 3.
    51→- [x] Variant (`erdos_84.variants.subexponential`) states `f(n) = o(2^n)` directly without `answer()`. Correct for Case 3.
    52→- N/A: Quantifier placement rule (only applies when `answer()` is used).
    53→
    54→## 7. Category Attribute
    55→
    56→- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
    57→- [x] Values are valid: `research open` for the main theorem, `research solved` for the variant.
    58→- [x] The `research open` theorem (`erdos_84`) contains `sorry` in its proof body.
    59→
    60→## 8. AMS Attribute
    61→
    62→- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
    63→- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed (`5`).
    64→- [x] Numbers are in ascending order (single number, trivially satisfied).
    65→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    66→- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.
    67→
    68→## 9. Proof Bodies
    69→
    70→- [x] Open conjecture (`erdos_84`) uses `by\n  sorry` (acceptable multi-line form).
    71→- [x] Solved variant also uses `by\n  sorry` (acceptable).
    72→- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
    73→- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open research problem.
    74→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    75→
    76→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    77→
    78→- [x] Variant is named `erdos_84.variants.subexponential` (correct `erdos_N.variants.<descriptor>` pattern).
    79→- [x] Variant lives in the **same file** as the main theorem.
    80→- [x] Variant has its own `@[category research solved, AMS 5]` attributes.
    81→- [x] Variant has a docstring explaining it is the solved part (Verstraëte [Ve04]).
    82→
    83→## 11. Code Quality
    84→
    85→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    86→- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`∀`, `∃`, `ℝ`, `ℕ`, `≥`, `≤`, `ε`).
    87→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    88→- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
    89→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
    90→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    91→
    92→## 12. Build Verification
    93→
    94→- [ ] `lake build` not verified in this review (requires build environment).

The two-part problem is cleanly split into a main `research open` theorem and a `research solved` variant with proper naming, attributes, and documentation. No issues found.

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/84.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 84`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/84`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (cycle sets, f(n), the conjecture).
- [x] Bibliography entries present: [Er94b], [Er95], [Er96], [Er97d], [Ve04].

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos84 ... end Erdos84`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos84`).
- [x] `open SimpleGraph` appears **before** the namespace block (line 45).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `cycleSpectrum` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- The cycle spectrum of a simple graph ... -/`).
- [x] `cycleSetCount` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- The number of distinct cycle spectra ... -/`).
- [x] `cycleSetCount` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `Set.ncard`).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Functions returning types use `lowerCamelCase` (`cycleSpectrum`, `cycleSetCount`).
- N/A: No custom types/Props defined, so UpperCamelCase rule does not apply.
- N/A: No local notation needed.
- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks, but this is optional ("consider").

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Both statements use the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_84` (snake_case matching namespace number).
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**.
- [x] Main theorem and variant both have docstrings.
- [x] Docstrings reference source papers: main cites [Er94b][Er95][Er96][Er97d]; variant cites [Ve04].

### `answer()` usage

The problem asserts two conjectures as direct facts ("Erdős and Faudree conjectured that f(n) = o(2^n) and f(n)/2^{n/2} → ∞"). This is **Case 3** — direct assertions. No `answer()` wrapper is needed.

- [x] Main theorem (`erdos_84`) states `f(n)/2^{n/2} → ∞` directly without `answer()`. Correct for Case 3.
- [x] Variant (`erdos_84.variants.subexponential`) states `f(n) = o(2^n)` directly without `answer()`. Correct for Case 3.
- N/A: Quantifier placement rule (only applies when `answer()` is used).

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
- [x] Values are valid: `research open` for the main theorem, `research solved` for the variant.
- [x] The `research open` theorem (`erdos_84`) contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed (`5`).
- [x] Numbers are in ascending order (single number, trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjecture (`erdos_84`) uses `by\n  sorry` (acceptable multi-line form).
- [x] Solved variant also uses `by\n  sorry` (acceptable).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variant is named `erdos_84.variants.subexponential` (correct `erdos_N.variants.<descriptor>` pattern).
- [x] Variant lives in the **same file** as the main theorem.
- [x] Variant has its own `@[category research solved, AMS 5]` attributes.
- [x] Variant has a docstring explaining it is the solved part (Verstraëte [Ve04]).

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`∀`, `∃`, `ℝ`, `ℕ`, `≥`, `≤`, `ε`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not verified in this review (requires build environment).
- [ ] Custom linter pass not verified in this review (requires build environment).

---

**Summary:** The file passes all style and structural checks. Definitions are well-docstringed and correctly marked `noncomputable`. The two-part problem is cleanly split into a main `research open` theorem and a `research solved` variant with proper naming, attributes, and documentation. No issues found.

content.text: The review summary says **"The file passes all style and structural checks. No issues found."** — this is a pass. No changes needed.
